'''Sam Lisone''' (born 19 February 1994), also known by the [[nickname]] of '''"Slammin"''',<ref name=bio>{{cite web|url=http://www.warriors.co.nz/team/profiles/sam-lisone.html|title=Sam Lisone|publisher=Warriors.co.nz|access-date=2015-05-03}}</ref> is a [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoa]] international [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|pr}} for [[Hull FC]] in the [[Betfred Super League]]

He previously played for the [[New Zealand Warriors]] and the [[Gold Coast Titans]] in the [[National Rugby League]] and the [[Leeds Rhinos]] in the [[Super League]]. Lisone has also played for the [[NRL All Stars team|NRL All Stars]] at representative level.

==Early life==
Lisone was born in [[Auckland, New Zealand]], and is of [[Samoans|Samoan]] and [[New Zealand people|New Zealand]] descent and attended [[Tangaroa College]].{{cn|date=July 2025}}

He played his junior rugby league for [[Otahuhu Leopards]].<ref name=bio/> He attended the National Secondary Schools competition in 2011 and was named in the tournament team.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.rleague.com/content/article.php?id=40887|title=NZRL National Secondary Schools Tournament - Awards| date=26 August 2011|access-date=15 June 2020|publisher=rleague.com|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20121102031622/http://www.rleague.com/content/article.php?id=40887|archive-date=2 November 2012}}</ref>

==Playing career==
Lisone was signed by the New Zealand Warriors in 2012 and played in the Warriors NYC team in 2013 and 2014. He attended an NRL rookie camp on 24 and 25 November 2012.<ref>{{cite web|date=24 November 2012|title=Junior Warriors at NRL Annual Rookie Camp|url=http://www.rleague.com/content/article.php?id=46138|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140322045021/http://rleague.com/news/Junior-Warriors-at-NRL-Annual-Rookie-Camp_75582/|archive-date=22 March 2014|access-date=7 March 2015}}</ref> He played in the [[2013 NRL Under-20s season|2013 Holden Cup]] Grand Final, which the club lost 30-42 to the [[Penrith Panthers]] juniors.

In 2014, Lisone also played in the [[New South Wales Cup]] for the Warriors and was 18th man for the first grade side on several occasions.<ref name=bio/> He co-captained the Junior Warriors in the [[2014 National Youth Competition (rugby league) season|2014 Holden Cup]] grand final, scoring a try in a 34-32 victory over the [[Brisbane Broncos]] juniors. He was named at prop in the [[NRL Under-20s|Holden Cup]] team of the year and also won the Warriors NYC Player of the Year and NYC Players’ Player of the Year awards.<ref name=awards>{{cite web|url=http://www.warriors.co.nz/news/2014/09/16/vodafone_warriors_red_ball_awards.html|title=Vodafone Warriors RED BALL Awards|date=16 September 2014 |publisher=Warriors.co.nz|access-date=2015-05-03}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|author=Steven Russo|url=http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/holden-team-year-announced/|title=Holden Cup Team Of The Year Announced|publisher=Rugbyleagueweek.com.au|date=2014-09-02|access-date=2015-05-03|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150626101814/http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/holden-team-year-announced/|archive-date=2015-06-26}}</ref> Lisone finished the year co-captaining the [[Junior Kiwis]] in a 15-14 victory over the Junior [[Australian Kangaroos|Kangaroos]] on 18 October.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/junior-kiwis-side-to-face-kangaroos/tabid/10874/newsid/82467/default.aspx|title=Junior Kiwis side to face Kangaroos|publisher=NRL.com|date=2014-10-10|access-date=2015-05-03}}</ref>
Lisone joined the New Zealand Warriors first grade squad for 2015, and was part of the squad for the [[2015 NRL Auckland Nines]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nzherald.co.nz/sport/news/article.cfm?c_id=4&objectid=11393099|title=League: Warriors confirm nines squad - Sport - NZ Herald News|date=28 January 2015 |publisher=Nzherald.co.nz|access-date=2015-05-03}}</ref>

Lisone made his first grade debut on 7 March 2015 against the [[Newcastle Knights]].<ref>{{cite web| url=http://www.warriors.co.nz/news/2015/03/07/nrl_team_news_two_pl.html| title=Two players ruled out| publisher=Warriors.co.nz| date=7 March 2015| access-date=7 March 2015}}</ref> He made 19 appearances for the Warriors in 2015, while mid-season his contract was extended until the end of 2018.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/solomone-kata-tuimoala-lolohea-and-sam-lisone-all-sign-contract-extensions-with-the-warriors/story-fni3gs8m-1227381820816|title=Solomone Kata, Tuimoala Lolohea and Sam Lisone all sign contract extensions with the Warriors|work=The Daily Telegraph|date=3 June 2015|access-date=11 January 2016}}</ref>
On 2 May 2015, Lisone played for [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoa]] against [[Tonga national rugby league team|Tonga]] in the 2015 [[Polynesian Cup]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/representative-round-team-lists/tabid/10874/newsid/85557/default.aspx|title=Representative Round team lists|publisher=NRL.com|date=2015-04-28|access-date=2015-05-03}}</ref>
On 2 May 2016, it was reported five players were stood down from an international test match for mixing prescription drugs with energy drinks on a night out.  The recipe of which can emulate the effect of illicit drugs.  Lisone was one of the players named who received a fine and was stood down.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/the-66-scandals-in-four-years-that-have-rocked-the-nrl/news-story/1bbcdffd432063bec8e521c736f7ae4d/|title=The 66 scandals in four years that have rocked the NRL|website=Fox Sports|date=8 February 2019 }}</ref>
On 8 October 2016, Lisone played for Samoa in their historical [[2016 Samoa vs. Fiji rugby league test match|test match]] against Fiji in Apia.Lisone played 16 games for the Gold Coast in the [[2021 NRL season]] including the club's elimination final loss against the [[Sydney Roosters]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2021/09/11/hes-taken-it-hard-shattered-titans-rally-around-herbert-after-blowing-win/|title='He's taken it hard': Shattered Titans rally around Herbert after blowing win|website=www.nrl.com|date=11 September 2021}}</ref>
Lisone played a total of 14 games for the Gold Coast in the [[2022 NRL season]] as the club finished 13th on the table.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2022-gold-coast-titans-season-review-justin-holbrook-tino-faasuamaleaui-david-fifita-toby-sexton-jayden-campbell-kieran-foran-jamal-fogarty/news-story/271373bd289c9c920124d2847fde6d5e/|title=Titans' million-dollar man goes missing as Holbrook rues costly mistake: Brutal Review|website=www.foxsports.com.au|date=8 September 2022 }}</ref>
Lisone played 23 games for Leeds in the 2023 Super League season as the club finished 8th on the table and missed the playoffs.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/leeds-rhinos-2023-season-review-picking-out-the-best-and-worst-players-performances-results-4355920/|title=Leeds Rhinos' 2023 season review: picking out the best and worst players, performances, results|website=www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk}}</ref>
Lisone played 23 games for Leeds in the 2024 Super League which saw the club finish 8th on the table.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/from-310-to-810-peter-smiths-leeds-rhinos-player-ratings-for-2024-season-4799226/|title=Leeds Rhinos player ratings from the 2024 Super League season|website=www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk}}</ref>
Lisone played 28 games for Leeds in the 2025 Super League season including their elimination playoff loss against St Helens.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/ce86r25lrxno/|title=Saints snatch stunning play-off victory at Leeds|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
